creative_brief: Split-screen: left side shows a cold, fluorescent-lit supermarket meat shelf with a '14-21 days in storage' label fading in; right side shows vibrant, freshly cut grass-fed beef on butcher paper with 'Days old' label. Warm, natural light on the right. FarmThru logo bottom-right.
primary_text:
14 to 21 days. That's how long supermarket beef sits in cold storage before you cook it.

At FarmThru, our beef is days old when you collect it. Rachel Ward's 100% grass-fed cattle graze regenerative pastures in Kempsey, NSW. No hormones. No antibiotics. No feedlots.

You can taste the difference when food hasn't travelled 2,400km in a refrigerated truck.

No middlemen. No wholesalers. Just real farms like Bundarra, Collins Wild Caught, and Farmer Brown sending food straight to our Brookvale hub.

Collect when it suits you, Monday to Friday. No commitment.
headline: Days old, not weeks old
description: Farm-direct beef, chicken, salmon and eggs. Collect from Brookvale, no lock-in.
